description: T7 tag — 11-residue N-terminal epitope (MASMTGGQQMG) from bacteriophage T7 Gene product 10A; used as a fusion tag for immunodetection and immunoaffinity purification.

references.title: Use of bacteriophage T7 RNA polymerase to direct selective high-level expression of cloned genes.

references.authors: Studier FW, Rosenberg AH, Dunn JJ, Dubendorff JW
references.title: Use of T7 RNA polymerase to direct expression of cloned genes.

Strategies for achieving high-level expression of genes in Escherichia coli.

label: T7_tag
note: T7 tag (gene 10 leader peptide): the N-terminal 11-residue epitope MASMTGGQQMG of bacteriophage T7 Gene product 10A (major capsid protein). Functions both as a translational leader in T7-based expression vectors and as a short immunological epitope tag recognised by anti-T7 monoclonal antibodies. Atomic: the 11 aa are the complete functional unit with no internal sub-features. Distinct from the upstream DNA 5' UTR RBS element (T7_g10_leader).

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:P19726 (CAPSA_BPT7, Major capsid protein / Gene product 10A, Escherichia phage T7; Swiss-Prot reviewed), positions 1–11 = MASMTGGQQMG; 100% identity over 11/11 aa, verified by direct efetch from rest.uniprot.org

functional_claims.label: The T7 tag (MASMTGGQQMG) is recognised by anti-T7 monoclonal antibodies, enabling immunodetection (Western blot, ELISA, immunoprecipitation, immunofluorescence) of T7-tagged fusion proteins.

value.mechanism: epitope tag — anti-T7 monoclonal antibody recognition

source.quote: T7 tag ... an 11 amino acid sequence ... detected by an antibody generated against bacteriophage T7 gene 10 protein ... used for detection and purification

functional_claims.label: The T7 tag functions as an epitope tag across multiple expression hosts including Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae; broader eukaryotic applicability is implied by its small size and non-cellular origin.

source.quote: Vectors for expressing T7 epitope- and His6 affinity-tagged fusion proteins in S. cerevisiae

functional_claims.label: Both natural products of T7 gene 10 (10A, 344 aa; and frameshifted 10B, 398 aa) share an identical N-terminal sequence MASMTGGQQMG, confirming the T7 tag as the shared, invariant N-terminus of both gp10 isoforms.

value.note: Both isoforms share identical N-terminus; frameshift affects only the C-terminal extension

source.quote: gene 10 of bacteriophage T7 is expressed as two proteins, 10A and 10B ... frameshifting ... both proteins share the same N terminus

functional_claims.label: The T7 tag sequence originates from the first 11 residues of the T7 gene 10 translation-initiation region, which was incorporated into T7-based expression vectors to drive strong translation of downstream cloned sequences.

value.mechanism: gene 10 leader used as translational enhancement element in T7 expression vectors
value.context: The peptide-encoding sequence immediately follows the T7 gene 10 Shine-Dalgarno; placing a cloned gene downstream appends the T7 tag to the recombinant protein N-terminus

source.quote: The first 260 nucleotides of the T7 gene 10, encoding about 40 amino acids, provide a translation-initiation signal and a portion of the gene 10 protein in front of the target gene
source.section: Methods in Enzymology, pET system description
